name: AI Knowledge Management
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for contextual retrieval, knowledge synthesis, expert Q&A, and content summarization

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput, multi-model routing, high concurrency

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Semantic search across knowledge base — hybrid vector + keyword retrieval, faceted navigation

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Knowledge articles, metadata, access patterns, version history, user interaction logs

prod.note: Dedicated throughput, single region, change feed

enterprise.note: Multi-region writes, automatic failover, analytical store

services.name: Microsoft Graph
services.purpose: SharePoint, Teams, OneDrive content ingestion — organizational knowledge graph traversal

prod.note: Included with M365 license, throttling limits apply

enterprise.note: Included with M365 E5, higher throttling limits

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Knowledge API — ingestion pipeline, retrieval orchestrator, content enrichment service

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Raw document storage — PDFs, Office docs, wiki exports, media files for processing

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys, Graph API credentials, encryption keys for sensitive knowledge content

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Search quality metrics, retrieval accuracy, query latency, knowledge freshness tracking

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for simple knowledge lookups and summaries, reserve GPT-4o for complex synthesis and cross-document reasoning — saves 65% on AI costs
Cache frequently accessed knowledge articles in Redis or in-memory with 15-minute TTL — reduces AI Search and OpenAI calls
Use Azure AI Search semantic ranker only for natural language queries — keyword-only queries skip the semantic tier and cost less
Implement incremental indexing via Cosmos DB change feed — avoids expensive full re-index operations
Use Microsoft Graph change notifications instead of polling — reduces API calls and improves freshness detection
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
